stem: In how many ways can [[n]] identical candies be distributed among [[k]] distinct children so that each child receives at least [[L]] candies?
solution: Give each child the required [[L]] candies first; the remaining \( [[n]] - [[k]]\cdot[[L]] = [[m]] \) candies are distributed freely, giving \( \binom{[[m]] + [[k]] - 1}{[[k]] - 1} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This ignores the minimum of [[L]] per child and applies stars and bars to all [[n]] candies.
This uses the wrong lower index \( k \) instead of \( k-1 \) for the bars.
This subtracts one too many from the reduced total.
This shifts the top of the binomial up by one.
This is off from the correct count by [[k]].
This is off from the correct count by [[L]].

stem: In how many ways can [[n]] identical candies be distributed among [[k]] distinct children so that each child receives AT MOST [[U]] candies?
solution: By inclusion-exclusion, start from the unrestricted count \( \binom{[[n]]+[[k]]-1}{[[k]]-1} \) and subtract the [[k]] ways one child could exceed the cap (receive at least \( [[U]]+1 \), reducing the problem to \( \binom{[[n]]-[[U]]-1+[[k]]-1}{[[k]]-1} \) free candies for that case): \( \binom{[[n]]+[[k]]-1}{[[k]]-1} - [[k]]\binom{[[n]]-[[U]]+[[k]]-2}{[[k]]-1} = [[answer]] \). No two children can simultaneously exceed the cap here, so no further correction is needed.
distractor_rationales: This ignores the upper bound entirely and applies plain stars and bars.
This reports only the raw over-count correction term without the k multiplier or the subtraction from the total.
This reports the full correction term but forgets to subtract it from the unrestricted total.
This forgets to multiply the correction term by the [[k]] ways to choose which child exceeds the cap.
This is off from the correct count by [[k]].
This is off from the correct count by [[U]].

stem: In how many ways can [[n]] identical candies be distributed among [[k]] distinct children so that each child receives at least [[L]] candies and at most [[U]] candies?
solution: Subtract the minimum [[L]] from each child first, leaving \( [[n]] - [[k]]\cdot[[L]] = [[n2]] \) candies to distribute so each child gets between 0 and \( [[U]]-[[L]] = [[W]] \) more. By inclusion-exclusion this is \( \binom{[[n2]]+[[k]]-1}{[[k]]-1} - [[k]]\binom{[[n2]]-[[W]]+[[k]]-2}{[[k]]-1} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This ignores both bounds and applies plain stars and bars to the full total [[n]].
This accounts for the lower bound shift but forgets the upper-bound cap correction entirely.
This reports only the raw over-count correction term, without subtracting it from the shifted total.
This forgets to multiply the correction term by the [[k]] ways to choose which child exceeds the cap.
This is off from the correct count by [[k]].
This is off from the correct count by [[L]].
